_comment: Test cases for the pdf-workspace LLM eval pipeline. Run with: python -m tests.eval.eval_chat --pdf-id <id>

single_turn.name: Category A — Orientation: broad document overview
single_turn.notes: Should produce 3-5 Big Ideas, not a chapter list. Hook must be a claim, not a description.

single_turn.name: Category A — Orientation: explicit summarise
single_turn.message: Summarise the main ideas of this document
single_turn.notes: Same as A_overview — confirm same template fires for 'summarise' phrasing.

single_turn.name: Category B — Mechanism: explain how something works
single_turn.message: How does the main concept in this document work? Walk me through the mechanism.
single_turn.notes: Layer 3 must read as a causal chain (because of that / which leads to), not a component list.

single_turn.name: Category C — Procedural: how do I steps
single_turn.message: What are the steps I should follow to apply what this document teaches?
single_turn.notes: Layer 3 must use numbered steps. Should name decision points or failure modes.

single_turn.name: Category D — Comparative: difference between concepts
single_turn.message: What is the difference between the key ideas discussed in this document?
single_turn.notes: Layer 3 must name the axis first. Two parallel descriptions without a named axis = fail.

single_turn.name: Category E — Evaluative: critique or assess
single_turn.message: What are the weaknesses or limitations of the arguments made in this document?
single_turn.notes: Must state the evaluative standard (what counts as a weakness) before the judgment.

single_turn.name: Category F — Application: give me an example
single_turn.message: Give me a concrete real-world example of how the ideas in this document apply in practice.
single_turn.notes: Example must be specific and chosen to reveal the principle, not just illustrate it.

single_turn.name: Highlight — full sentence selection
single_turn._selection_text_note: Will be filled at runtime from first RAG chunk of the PDF
single_turn.notes: Frame (Layer 2) must mention the section title. Full highlight = full depth response.

single_turn.name: Structure check — Deep Dive heading always present
single_turn.message: Give me a quick overview of this document
single_turn.notes: The heading must appear verbatim. Even for a quick/vague question.

multi_turn.name: Multi-turn — conciseness follow-up

turns.message: Explain the main argument of this document in depth
That's too detailed. Give me the single most important takeaway in 2–3 sentences.

assertions.note: Response must be meaningfully shorter (< 75%) than turn 0. Absolute cap raised to 200 because the template itself adds some fixed overhead.

multi_turn.notes: Tests that the model adapts depth within a conversation, not just restates.

multi_turn.name: Multi-turn — follow-up clarification

turns.message: What is the core framework presented in this document?
Can you go deeper on the first point you mentioned?

assertions.note: Response must build on, not repeat, the first turn.

multi_turn.notes: Tests coherent multi-turn — second response must reference what was said in turn 1.

multi_turn.name: Multi-turn — escalate from highlight to full explain

turns.message: Now explain the broader context this passage sits in

assertions._note: is_longer_than_turn removed — depends on which passage was selected and is flaky

multi_turn.notes: Second turn drops selection context — should produce a broader, longer response.
